title: Centos 8 install error
body:
/kind bug

**package checksum errors from kubic repo trying to install on Centos 8**

`[MIRROR] containers-common-1-6.el8.noarch.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 00a3c53eeff9c1144b0b3fa95dd528289cfa3bdb7ba3ff407fdfcf6186afffd4(sha256)  Expected: 00d1ca496f30fca20de3440855a6ffc355b3c61b5c74cd12ef051188792b01eb(sha256)`

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ` docker build -t centos-podman .` using the following Dockerfile

```
FROM centos:8

RUN echo "[INFO] Installing Container Tools" \
    && dnf -y module disable container-tools \
    && dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)' \
    && dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux \
    && curl -sSL -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo \
    && dnf clean all \
    && dnf -y install podman skopeo buildah

RUN echo "[INFO] Testing container tools]" \
    && podman --storage-driver=vfs info \
    && echo yes | podman --storage-driver=vfs system reset \
    && podman --storage-driver=vfs --version \
    && buildah --storage-driver=vfs --version \
    && skopeo --version
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```

**Describe the results you expected:**

expected successful installation of podman, buildah, skopeo

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

this problem has come and gone with various packages in the kubic repo over the past year or so

**Output of `podman version`:**

n/a -- install failed

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

n/a -- install failed


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

n/a -- install failed


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Docker build `FROM centos:8`

comments.body: [similar issues](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues?q=kubic+checksum) have come and gone over the past year or so:

Tried centos\_stream and get a checksum error on a different package:

```
[MIRROR] catatonit-0.1.5-4.el8.x86_64.rpm: Interrupted by header callback: Server reports Content-Length: 297180 but expected size is: 297188
```

Dockerfile to reproduce with centos stream:

```
#ARG BASE="centos:8"
ARG BASE="quay.io/centos/centos:stream8"
FROM ${BASE}
#ARG KUBIC="https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo"
ARG KUBIC="https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8_Stream/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo"
RUN echo "[INFO] Installing Container Tools" \
   && dnf -y module disable container-tools \
   && dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)' \
   && dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux \
   && curl -sSL -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo ${KUBIC} \
   && dnf clean all \
   && echo starting install... \
   && dnf -y install podman skopeo buildah
RUN echo "[INFO] Testing container tools]" \
   && podman --storage-driver=vfs info \
   && echo yes | podman --storage-driver=vfs system reset \
   && podman --storage-driver=vfs --version \
   && buildah --storage-driver=vfs --version \
   && skopeo --version
```

If the issues continue to persist with OBS, I'm thinking I'll start sending packages to fedora copr for all centos builds. Anyway, please give me a few days to sort things out with the OBS people.
thanks @lsm5 -- let me know anything I can do if you need something tested etc.

Behavior has changed...

Docker build FROM centos:8 now builds without errors.

Docker build FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream8 is now reporting checksum error on a different package (buildah):

```
[MIRROR] buildah-1.19.6-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 2feb38c97bf9b33913792e79d7e8b2e5b4897373099a93a649b4331a5afda031(sha256)  Expected: 0ced5ce93752002e4d6173da7d7c9e7d63be30aefc1ecfd224661303e1cb50bb(sha256)
```

Hope this helps...
@lmgray ack, sent a buildah rebuild just now. Please give it maybe an hour or so.

@lmgray had some insight from the OBS people earlier today and I'm trying something on the "testing" subproject, I'll migrate changes to the repos you use above once things are stable. By "stable", I mean the installation works fine, though random issues like checksum mismatch can only be noticed over some time with newer builds/rebuilds. :crossed_fingers:
we build our base image twice per day (to stay ahead of vulnerabilities etc.) so we're a good test of problems that develop over time with newer builds/rebuilds -- thanks for investigating!
@lmgray You misspelled Podman above with "d o c k e r"?

sorry -- not understanding @rhatdan ...   are you suggesting these intermittent problems are related to building the container using docker vs. using podman for the build?  I would assume the packages are the same whether it's a non-container install (e.g. installing podman to a CentOS VM) or a container and should not matter what container build tool I'm using...   The Dockerfiles I included were just to make reproducing the problem easy.

What am I missing?

for completeness, here's the issue demonstrated using podman:

```
